page: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor with 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores, utilizing 2 threads per core at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor includes a 32 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mix of top-tier single-core capabilities and average multi-core throughput. The instance achieves top-tier (top 10%) results in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, because the CPU allocation is shared, multi-core benchmarks like stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and the PassMark CPU score fall into the average (middle 50%) category. Memory performance is strong, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, while large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU architecture allows Vultr to offer a substantial 128.0 GB memory footprint at a lower resource tier, making it highly qualitative for memory-heavy workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core CPU performance. Tradeoffs include the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under noisy-neighbor conditions, and the minimal 1 GB network storage. This server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale caching, in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and lightweight LLM text generation using small models, where single-threaded execution speeds and high RAM capacity are prioritized over raw multi-threaded compute power.
description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 shared x86_64 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC processor and 128.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.0 GHz across 8 physical cores, the instance provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It includes a 1 GB network storage volume and no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong memory performance, though shared CPU allocation limits multi-core benchmarks to average levels. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy workloads like Redis or small-model LLM text generation.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128gb is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized shared instance combining a massive 128 GB RAM footprint with top-tier single-core execution speeds for caching and databases.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
1 GB network-attached local storage and no GPU accelerators
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis, and caching layers
